sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 5.33 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.5)

statement.latex: (Y.\,Ihara). Consider the quaternion algebra$Q$with norm$f=x^2 - y^2 - z^2 + u^2$,$, { Z}$. Assume that$f$is indefinite and of${ Q}$-rank 0, i.~e.$f=0$for$x,y,z,u { Q}$implies$x=y=z=u=0$. Consider$Q$as the algebra of the matrices$$X= (\!\! {cc}x+ y& (z+ u) z- u&x- y \!\! )$$with$x,y,z,u { Q}$. Let$p$be a prime,$p$.
